data.body:
Session: diagnosed + fixed ticket-store concurrency corruption; closed epic REB-601.

## 1. Closed epic REB-601 (main-name-crowd) through the completion verifier
- All 17 children were closed, but the close was BLOCKED: two children (diss-ale-jet/ce94,
  seedy-edema-groan/b043) had been FORCE_CLOSE'd (Gerrit merges) with no completion signature,
  violating the epic AC ("children closed with signed completion verdicts").
- Re-closed both through the verifier to earn signatures (diss-ale-jet is a bug → needed
  "Fixed:" reason; seedy hit a transient 429, retried). Then re-closed REB-601 → PASS.
- Note: REB-601 closed but UNSIGNED (certifiable:false) because 13/17 children were historically
  closed without completion signatures. Closed on its own criteria; not certified.

## 2. Filed bug f193 (saggy-pupil-plant) for the concurrency corruption + ran /joe-debug

Symptom: `rebar fsck` reported ~2929 issues (2398 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT, 530 ORPHAN_EVENT,
1 MISSING_CREATE); b043 read `open` while carrying a completion-verifier signature.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSES (3 distinct; flock is local-only and never covered these cross-machine paths):
- RC1 (SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT): the Jira reconciler CI workflows (reconcile-bridge.yml +
  reconcile-bridge-canary.yml) fetched the tickets branch shallow (--depth=1) and reconciled
  with `git rebase`. Shallow graft defeats git merge-base → rebase re-applies the reconciler's
  stale pre-compaction worktree over a laptop COMPACT commit, resurrecting the source event
  files compaction deleted while keeping the SNAPSHOT. Proven by production git artifact
  (commit 51e0a578c single-parent off COMPACT 3afe230a0, re-adds 8 byte-identical deleted
  files; 8-present/2-absent = stale-fetch-boundary fingerprint) AND a controlled experiment
  (only shallow+rebase corrupts; full+merge and full+rebase clean; shallow+merge fails safe
  rc=128). Deeper enabler: compaction hard-os.remove()s folded files (compact.py:142) instead
  of the documented `*.retired` rename (invariant I1, docs/concurrency.md:35-36,154 — which
  explicitly says "merge unions cleanly, rebase conflicts"). `.retired` was never implemented.
- RC2a (ORPHAN_EVENT false positives, ~519): compaction folds only KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES but fsck
  flagged ANY pre-snapshot event absent from source_event_uuids → REVIEW_RESULT (reducer-ignored)
  falsely flagged. Deterministic, single-clone.
- RC2b (ORPHAN_EVENT genuine, ~11 remaining): concurrent write on a stale base gets an HLC ts
  below a peer's snapshot horizon; a clean merge keeps it as a pre-snapshot event that replay
  silently skips → real data loss (invariant I9 unsatisfiable across un-synced clones).
- Correction: the b043 "open + signature" symptom was NOT missing-event corruption — it was a
  legitimate reconciler STATUS closed→open (Jira-authoritative reopen).

Method: 3 parallel investigators (code-tracer, historical, empirical) + my own git-artifact
verification + a controlled shallow/full × merge/rebase reproduction matrix.

## 3. Shipped fixes #1 + #3 (LANDED on main via Gerrit change 318, commit 5a7548a, MERGED)
- Fix #1: both reconciler workflows → full history (fetch-depth: 0, no --depth=1) + `git merge
  --no-edit` (not rebase). Guard test: tests/interfaces/store/test_reconcile_workflow_no_shallow_rebase.py.
- Fix #3: fsck._check_snapshot exempts non-KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES from ORPHAN_EVENT (symmetric with
  compaction) while still flagging genuine KNOWN-type orphans. Tests:
  tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py (RED→GREEN + mutation-tested).
- End-to-end replication: live-store ORPHAN_EVENT dropped 530 → 11 (11 remaining are genuine
  KNOWN-type -EDIT.json orphans). SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT keeps growing until fix #1 DEPLOYS
  (next reconcile run on new main); the ~2400 existing inconsistencies still need remediation.
- Both Gerrit gates green (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1); submitted → merged → replicated to GitHub.

## 4. Created placeholder epic adept-hedge-stain (ace2-f784-4b19-4f22) for deferred work
- #2 implement `*.retired` non-destructive compaction (durable class-fix for RC1)
- #4 snapshot-horizon-safe reconciliation (RC2b data loss)
- #5 remediate the already-corrupted live store (drive fsck to zero; gated on #1 deploy)
Linked discovered_from f193.

## 5. Env fix
- The .venv was an editable install with stale metadata (0.5.2) and missing dev tools (no mypy,
  which blocked the pre-commit typecheck gate). `make install` (venv active) → nava-rebar 0.7.0
  editable + mypy/coverage/grimp/pytest-cov + re-wired hook. The `unknown key` config warning is gone.
- Chose `make install` over `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.0` (the released wheel would strip the
  editable install + the local fix and contradict dev-env guidance).

## Gotchas for next session
- This shared checkout is contended: a concurrent session switched it to branch
  feature/completion-file-impact-commit-gate mid-work (working tree briefly showed pre-fix
  content). My change was already committed + landed server-side via Gerrit, so nothing was lost.
- Shell state (venv activation) does NOT persist between tool calls — `source .venv/bin/activate`
  in every command, or make install/commit hooks use system python and fail (PEP 668 / missing mypy).
- Gerrit REST as JoeOakhartNava: fetch cred with username pinned, then curl -u; submit =
  POST /a/changes/<n>/submit.
